transcript.speaker: Vinod
transcript.text: Good afternoon everyone. Okay, let's get started. Today is the Sprint 4 kickoff call — sprint starts Monday. I'll be direct upfront. Client has confirmed that the Payment API go-live is their number one priority. They've communicated it internally to their payments team and operations team. The date they expect is Wednesday, May 20. That is a committed date. Rupali, walk us through the plan.

transcript.speaker: Rupali
transcript.text: Thank you sir. Sprint 4 scope is the Payment API end to end — gateway integration, payment flow frontend, full QA coverage including edge cases, and production deployment. We have 10 working days starting Monday. Wednesday May 20 as go-live means we have roughly Day 1 through Day 3 to build and test, with deployment on Day 3. That's tight but achievable if we start clean on Monday. Rakesh, where is the development side right now?

transcript.speaker: Rakesh
transcript.text: Core payment API development is essentially done — the integration logic, initiation, success handling, failure handling. Code is reviewed and merged. The one concern going into Monday is something Varsha has been tracking — the payment gateway sandbox credentials. Client sent them last Tuesday and we have two endpoint URLs in their integration doc. We haven't confirmed which one is the correct sandbox endpoint. That needs to be verified Day 1 so we're not wasting testing time hitting the wrong URL.

transcript.speaker: Varsha
transcript.text: Yes — I'll verify the endpoint first thing Monday morning and share the confirmed API response schema with Suraj. She needs it for the confirmation page field mapping. I'll aim to have it to her by noon Monday.

transcript.speaker: Suraj
transcript.text: That's what I need. Payment confirmation page is mostly built but right now the field mapping is based on assumptions. I also have a small mobile alignment issue I spotted — maybe 2 hours of work. I'll take care of both Monday.

transcript.speaker: Vinod
transcript.text: Suraj — the alignment issue. Client will look at this on their phones. Get it done Monday, not Tuesday.

transcript.speaker: Suraj
transcript.text: Understood sir, first thing Monday.

transcript.speaker: Rupali
transcript.text: Vivek, I want to raise the staging environment before we go further. What's the current status?

transcript.speaker: Vivek
transcript.text: That's risk number one I want to flag. Staging is still pointing to the old database config from Sprint 3. I raised the infra ticket earlier this week and I followed up today — central infra hasn't applied it yet. I'll chase them again this afternoon and over the weekend if needed. My goal is to have it resolved before Monday's 9am standup. But it is dependent on central infra responding, which I can't fully control.

transcript.speaker: Ajay
transcript.text: That environment issue is a hard blocker for testing. I have 34 test cases written and ready for the payment flow. But if staging isn't working Monday, I can't run a single one. Any delay there directly compresses the testing window against a Wednesday go-live.

transcript.speaker: Vivek
transcript.text: Understood. I'll be available this weekend to push infra if needed. There's also one other thing I want to flag now so it's not a surprise later — I want to verify whether the prod SSL certificate authority matches staging. Central infra manages prod separately. We've seen config drift between environments in previous sprints and cert mismatches can cause API calls to fail. I'll add that to my Monday morning checklist.

transcript.speaker: Rakesh
transcript.text: Good call Vivek. Check it early Monday, not mid-week. And actually — while we're on environment risks — can we be crystal clear about which staging environment the client sees for demos? We have staging-v1 and staging-v2 and historically there's been confusion about which one is active.

transcript.speaker: Vivek
transcript.text: I'll send a clear written note to the team Sunday evening. Which environment is for internal testing, which one is client-facing for demos. No ambiguity going into Monday.

transcript.speaker: Ajay
transcript.text: Also — if a client demo is scheduled this week, can we agree on a 5-minute pre-demo check? Environment, latest deployment version, one smoke test result. Nothing elaborate. Just so we're not showing the client something that hasn't been verified in the last hour.

transcript.speaker: Rakesh
transcript.text: Absolutely. Pre-demo verification checklist. We'll establish that. Good thinking Ajay.

transcript.speaker: Vinod
transcript.text: Let me be clear about expectations for the week. Wednesday May 20 is the go-live date. Client has already told their internal teams. If we slip that date they escalate to the BU head. I need a daily status update from Rupali by noon each day — not just standup summaries, a direct message to me. Staging issue needs to be resolved by Monday morning, no excuses. And if anything blocks us, raise it immediately. Don't sit on a problem.

transcript.speaker: Rupali
transcript.text: Understood sir. Daily noon updates from Monday. Vivek — raise the infra ticket with Vinod sir escalation on it if you haven't already. It usually speeds up response.

transcript.speaker: Vivek
transcript.text: I'll update the ticket now to include Vinod sir's name as escalation point.

transcript.speaker: Rakesh
transcript.text: One last thing from me. This is a go-live sprint. The risk profile is different from a development sprint. Please raise blockers early — not at end of day, not in tomorrow's standup. In the group chat the moment you hit something you can't resolve in 30 minutes. That's how we protect the Wednesday date.

transcript.speaker: Rupali
transcript.text: Agreed. Okay everyone — have a good weekend. Let's start Sprint 4 clean on Monday. We know what needs to happen. Let's make it happen.
